---
title: IMG/M
alias: &ALIAS imgm
href: https://img.jgi.doe.gov/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Integrated Microbial Genomes & Microbiomes (IMG/M) system from the Joint Genome Institute (JGI/DOE) supports analysis of microbial genomes and microbiome datasets in a comparative genomics context.

## Key Features

- Integrates microbial genomes from isolates, single cells, and metagenomes in a unified system
- Provides gene annotation, functional classification, and metabolic pathway analysis tools
- Supports comparative genomics across thousands of microbial genomes and microbiomes
- Regularly updated with JGI sequencing project outputs and community-contributed data
- Offers advanced query tools for gene content, COG/KEGG annotation, and phylogenetic context

## Use Cases in Research

- Analyzing AMR gene distributions across microbial genomes and environmental microbiomes
- Comparing metabolic potential of microbiome communities across health and disease states
- Identifying gene clusters and biosynthetic pathways in novel microbial isolate genomes
- Accessing DOE-sequenced environmental metagenomes for ecological microbiome studies
- Contextualizing novel resistance genes within broader microbial genome neighborhoods

## Getting Started

Visit [IMG/M](https://img.jgi.doe.gov/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
